Name: curvature_aware_mixed_precision
Title: Curvature-Aware Mixed-Precision Quantization for Deep Neural Networks
Short Hypothesis: We hypothesize that incorporating curvature-aware precision routing into mixed-precision quantization can significantly improve the efficiency and accuracy of deep neural networks, particularly in resource-constrained devices.
Related Work: Recent works such as EMQ, SNIP, and AMP-ViT have explored mixed-precision quantization and adaptive precision routing. However, they often rely on heuristic-based methods or require significant computational resources. Our proposal differs from these works by incorporating curvature-aware precision routing, which provides a more principled and efficient approach to mixed-precision quantization.
Abstract: This proposal introduces a novel curvature-aware mixed-precision quantization framework for deep neural networks. By incorporating curvature-aware precision routing, our framework can efficiently allocate precision levels to different layers and components of the network, minimizing computational resources while maintaining accuracy. We evaluate our framework on various benchmark datasets and demonstrate significant improvements in efficiency and accuracy compared to state-of-the-art mixed-precision quantization methods.
Experiments: Evaluate the proposed curvature-aware mixed-precision quantization framework on benchmark datasets such as ImageNet and CIFAR-10
Compare the performance of our framework with state-of-the-art mixed-precision quantization methods such as EMQ, SNIP, and AMP-ViT
Investigate the impact of curvature-aware precision routing on the efficiency and accuracy of deep neural networks
Explore the application of our framework to resource-constrained devices such as edge devices and mobile devices
Risk Factors and Limitations: The proposed framework may require significant computational resources for training and evaluation
The curvature-aware precision routing algorithm may not always converge to the optimal solution
The framework may not be compatible with all deep neural network architectures and datasets
